item.title: 公投可以成為美國的第51州？全球公投的發展史The History of Global Referendums|#我的學習筆記 #522 @mynotebooks
item.itunes:title: 公投可以成為美國的第51州？全球公投的發展史The History of Global Referendums|#我的學習筆記 #522 @mynotebooks
item.itunes:author: 我的學習筆記
item.itunes:summary:
Hello! Hello, everyone! I'm a niu ge. We've talked about the abolitionist controversy before. But in fact, in the Republic of China. There is no conclusion on the abolition of the death penalty! Chiu Siu-hong of the KMT even said that. A referendum should be held to decide to abolish the death penalty! And at about the same time. Vietnam also on April 23rd. for “inciting subversion of human rights”. Sentencing of 10 Defendants. 4 to 13 years imprisonment. The reason is that these people are calling for a referendum. To determine the future of Vietnam. A referendum is a way of realizing the will of the people. It's also a demonstration of direct democracy. Voters in a democracy. It's like a constitutional amendment,. Sovereignty, and a social system. Important public issues like this. can be resolved by referendum. Today we're going to introduce. Referendums under different social systems. Let's start with the old countries. Britain's Brexit Referendum. Starting with the series of referendums it triggered. We're going to talk about the referendums. Then we have to talk about the United Kingdom. When Britain was in the process of leaving the European Union. The referendum held in 2016. This referendum is not mandatory. But it's still the result of public opinion. It's been presented to the public. The EU Withdrawal Bill. Under this bill. Britain will leave the EU in 2019. So why does the UK want to leave the EU? That's a pretty big question. It covers political, economic and social aspects. It's like someone might think. Britain has lost a lot of autonomy and decision-making power. Because it had to be obeyed. Some of the regulations and policies set by the European Union. They believe that EU laws and regulations. have too much influence on British policy making. and limited the ability of the state to make its own decisions. Or the European Union's free movement policy. AKA citizens of the EU. Can move freely within the European Union. Dissatisfaction with work and residence. Thinking that it leads to too many immigrants. Affects employment opportunities for locals. And that's what most of the Brits who supported Brexit did. That's the main reason why they support Brexit. In England and Wales. People are more likely to support Brexit. But Scotland and Northern Ireland. Most people want to stay in the EU. Leaders in Northern Ireland. And even decided to take Northern Ireland. Is it going to leave the United Kingdom? A referendum was also held. History. Northern Ireland has also been criticized for. The question of whether or not to accept British jurisdiction. The two sides have been in turmoil for a long time. 1998. Belfast Agreement and EU Accession.

It's really over. 30 years of violent conflict. Now Britain is out of the European Union. Triggering the terms of the Belfast Agreement. There's a clause in this agreement that says. If it is clear that the majority of the people. Of Northern Ireland want to. Northern Ireland splits from Britain. The British and Irish Governments. A referendum should be organized. The Other Side of England. Scotland is still seeking a referendum on independence. Back in 2014. Scotland for the first time. Referendum on Independence. The result was 44.2% to 55.8%. Failed to declare independence. There is still a group of people in Scotland. looking for a way out of Britain. June 2022. Scottish Local Government Program. Second Independence Referendum in 2023. Rejected by British Prime Minister Johnson. Supreme Court Ruling. Not Authorized by the UK Government. Scotland is not authorized to. A referendum on leaving the United Kingdom. But unlike Northern Ireland and Scotland. Gibraltar, also a British territory. They wanted to stay in the UK through a referendum. Gibraltar is a British overseas territory. It's only about 6 square kilometers in size. The landmass is surrounded by Spain. Spain keeps claiming. He had sovereignty over Gibraltar. 1954. Spain restricts water supply to Gibraltar. Trying to regain sovereignty over Gibraltar. The result was that in 1967. Gibraltar held its first ever referendum. The result was in favor of the transfer of sovereignty to Spain. Only 44 votes. And those in favor of British sovereignty. There are 12,138 votes. After this. The British government also tried to. To return Gibraltar to Spain. Resulting in the return of Gibraltar to Spain in 2002. Another referendum was held. But in the end, it ended in. 98.97% of the votes were against the motion. Allowed Britain to maintain its rule in Gibraltar. But in fact, Britain was. It's made up of four different countries. and overseas territories. All of these places have the right to pass a referendum. to decide whether or not they want to stay in the UK. Just like the UK's referendum on leaving the EU. I'm done with Britain. Let's talk about it. Referendums in other democracies. Actually, something similar. It happened in Puerto Rico, in the Caribbean. As early as 1898. The U.S. was in the Spanish-American War. After winning the Spanish War. This former Spanish colony.

It fell into American hands. And then in 1917. Legislation was enacted and then formalized. Puerto Ricans are U.S. citizens. And also to help Puerto Rico set up a system of government. Including the Governor, the chief executive, who is directly elected by the people. And the bicameral legislature. 139 00:05:07,557 --> 00:05:09,767. Puerto Rico has a state militia. And armed forces similar to the state guard. Includes land, sea, and air forces. However. Restricted by the legal position of belonging. Puerto Rico in the U.S. Congress. There are no senators. There's only one non-voting delegate. Although Puerto Ricans don't have to carry passports. They can move freely throughout the U.S. States. You can also register as a voter in each state. But as long as the person is in Puerto Rico. You can't vote in the presidential election. You can only express your opinion in the party primaries. And candidates are usually here. After writing the policy check. I'm going to bring the campaign back to the home states. It's been over 100 years. American Puerto Ricans. Empty Citizenship. Without the full rights of citizenship. Except for the right to vote. Their local income. No Federal Income Tax. But you can't apply for it either. Some Federal Social Welfare Assistance. It's about becoming a state of the United States. It's better to be independent. It's been a controversial topic for decades. The idea of “independence” or “statehood”. Also a different local party. Key issues that need to be addressed. Last seen in November 2020. With 53% of voters. In favor of becoming a U.S. state. 47% of voters oppose. Deciding whether to become the 51st state of the United States. A slight majority in favor. Similar to the trend over the years. But this result. It needs to be passed by the U.S. Congress. To become a state of the United States. So these referendums. They're not legally enforceable. It only has the effect of tabula rasa. It's just a reference for Congress. 185 00:06:40,983 --> 00:06:42,819. But in December of 2022. Just before the end of the congressional session. The House of Representatives passed the Puerto Rico Status Act. The bill proposes that Puerto Rico. Hold Another Referendum. To determine the political positioning of Puerto Rico. There are three options. Become a state of the United States. Independence. Or after independence. Free association with the United States. The point is. For the first time in history. Giving mandatory force to the referendum result.

Whatever the outcome, the U.S. Congress. The choice of the Puerto Ricans will be honored. Let's move on to the next point. Another interesting country. That's Quebec, Canada. Quebec, Canada. Actually, something similar happened! What makes Quebec so special. A colonial history of the past. It's a very close relationship. The major ethnic groups in Quebec. are Catholics of French descent. With the majority of other Christian Englishmen. In language, culture, religion, and blood. There is a clear difference. Originally Canada's Constitution Act 1982. Bypassing Quebec for Constitutional Amendments. Quebec Considers New Constitution. does not protect the rights of the French-speaking population. Quebec is only considered a province of Canada. No special status for “Britain and France”. Then Premier of Quebec. Lévesque refused to sign the Canadian Constitution. Although the court said. that the Canadian Constitution applies automatically. To the entire country. But the federal government, in order to resolve this. A constitutional crisis involving Quebec's feelings of independence. Negotiations with the provinces for a constitutional amendment. The 1987 Lake Michigan Accord. The Canadian government delegates a lot of power. Including the power to veto constitutional amendments. Nomination of Supreme Court Judges. Developing Immigration Policy. Financial Aid and More. Since Quebec is not the only one to benefit from the program. The premiers of the provinces. Also accepted these proposals with open arms. The Parti Québécois, which advocates for Quebec's independence. In the 1994 Quebec general election. Won the Ruling Party. After the Québécois came to power. And then this referendum was called. In October of 1995. On “Whether or not Quebec secedes from Canada. Becoming an independent country. A referendum was held. Result 49.42% of voters. Recognized that Quebec should secede from Canada. 50.58% of voters oppose. In a democracy. Citizens can pass a referendum. Deciding whether to secede from a country. Or join another country. The people who live in this land. Have the right to self-determination by referendum. To decide on this piece of land. Sovereignty, social system. But another kind of social system. is not so lucky. Since there is a normal, legal procedure for referendums. Of course there's coerced referendums. And now for the next part. The referendum on Crimea. Early 2014. Russia sends unidentified militants. Occupied Ukraine.

After the Crimean Autonomous Republic. A referendum was also held. It was decided that Crimea would remain in Ukraine. Or to join Russia. There are two questions in the referendum. First. Do you support Crimea? To become a territory of the Russian Federation. Second. Are you in favor of resuming. Crimea's 1992 Constitution. Both options are to secede from Ukraine. But Ukraine and most of the UN member states 277. do not recognize the referendum. Considers it a violation of Ukraine's territorial integrity. and adopted United Nations General Assembly Resolution 68/262. Recognizing the referendum as null and void. Calling on countries not to recognize. The result of this referendum. February 2022. Russia invades Ukraine. Occupation of Ukrainian territory. Donetsk, Luhansk. Zaporozhye, Kherson. September 2022. Russia has initiated referendums in these four cities. At the time of the referendum. Military action between Russia and Ukraine. It's still going on. Most of the population of the four cities. have fled. The U.N. condemns the referendum as a violation. Of the U.N. Charter. There is only one country, North Korea. Accepted the validity of the referendum result. No other sovereign state accepts it. Finished with the West. Let's talk about the Chinese world. Actually, it's something like this. A referendum on national sovereignty. Not only in the Western world! In fact, there are referendums in the Chinese world as well. It's like after the end of World War II. Britain was in great decline. The British government is seriously considering. From Southeast Asia, including Singapore. Making a Gorgeous Turn. September 1962. Singapore was held. Referendum on Accession to the Articles of Confederation of Malaysia. The referendum was initiated by then Prime Minister Lee Kuan Yew. The referendum offered three choices. 1. Partial Autonomy. 2. Full Merger. 3. Reference to the treatment of East Malaysia. In the end, 71% of the voters chose. Partial autonomy option with maximum autonomy. According to the result of the referendum. Singapore in September 1963. Formal merger with Malaysia. Marking the birth of the Federation of Malaysia. Until 1965. Singapore was expelled from the Federation of Malaysia. Becoming an independent country. But that's another story. It was also a British colony. The Hong Kong government has held official referendums.

Some political parties have also organized unofficial referendums. So let's start with the official referendum. Early Hong Kong. Having a Council Outside the Government. Responsible for cleaning up Hong Kong. That's the Cleaning Authority. That's the predecessor of the Urban Council. The Cleansing Bureau was established as early as 1883. In the 19th century. To be responsible for the hygiene and prevention. Of epidemic in Hong Kong. It was also one of the few. Elected Public Bodies. But in fact, in the early days of its existence. The Cleansing Authority was also an official governmental. Controlled by Members of the House of Representatives. A battleground between the government and the citizenry. This tug-of-war eventually forced the authorities to. In 1896. In the case of the Cleansing Authority,. It should be guarded by the Crown. or unofficial Members in the majority. Holding a referendum. The result is a large majority of voters. Agree that the Clean Council should be dominated. By unofficial members. Although the voting result is different from the government's idea. The Cleansing Authority had no choice but to maintain. Of the six non-official members. 2 were directly elected. This referendum. Hong Kong people for the first time. Trying out the experience of elected municipal councils. Another Referendum in Hong Kong. It's the Civic Party of Hong Kong. And the League of Social Democrats. The two political parties in 2009. To fight for genuine universal suffrage. A political movement for the realization of universal suffrage. In the five Hong Kong Legislative Council constituencies. One pan-democrat in each district. Resignation of Members of the Legislative Council of Hong Kong. Creation of Five Vacant Seats. and then in accordance. With the Hong Kong Legislative Council Ordinance. A by-election must be held. The League of Social Democrats and the Civic Party. Proposed in the Legislative Council. “Motion on ”Resignation en masse of Members returned from five geographical constituencies as a referendum. Motion ultimately defeated. January 2010. Formal Resignation of Five Legislative Councillors. April of the same year. Five Resigning Members Re-apply for Election. The result is that the five resigning Members. Winning by a large margin of votes. Returned to the Legislative Council. Resignation en masse in five districts. Voting on the issue of “universal suffrage. Five Resigning Members. Returning to the Legislative Council. With a large percentage of votes. Considered as public opinion in favor of genuine universal suffrage. Also extremely controversial. Although Hong Kong has not legislated for referendum. But there have been referendums. In both the government and the public.

According to the UN Charter and international law. In the absence of external oppression or interference. The people are free to decide. Their political status. And free to pursue their economic. Social and cultural development. It's also what we usually call the right to self-determination. So much for that. Finally, let's go back to the Republic of China. Unlike the colonial society of Hong Kong. When the Republic of China was founded. In fact, it gave citizens the right to vote. It's a kind of referendum like in Western politics. A direct decision by the people. Modifying or Repealing a Law. A power to administer the law. But it's not very specific. So it's been a long time in dispute. The Constitution of the Republic. Of China is a mere article in the Constitution. No clear procedure for implementation. In 2018. After amendment of the Referendum Act. Returning the Right to Vote to Referendum. Current Taiwan. Already on the issue of people's livelihood. 16 referendums have been held. Including the most recent 16th case. For the purpose of whether to repeal Article 95(1) of the Electricity Law. That is to say, to abolish “nuclear power generating facilities”. Before the 114th year of the Republic of China. The “Stop All Operations” Requirement. 54.83% turnout. Of which 29.84% agree. Finally passed. Other topics are like. In forms other than civil marriage. Protecting Same-Sex Couples. The right to operate a permanent joint life. Under the full name of (Taiwan). Apply for all international sports events. and 2020 Tokyo Olympics. Prohibition of the opening of Fukushima,. 311 Importation of Agricultural Products and Foodstuffs. From Nuclear Disaster-Related Areas. And the subject of these. All of these issues have been put to a referendum. In the Republic of China. So in the R.O.C. From energy, people's livelihood, law, and other issues. All of them can be put to a referendum. The independence of Taiwan. Couldn't it also be realized through a referendum? And would it be unconstitutional or not? If you're interested. We'll come back to this later. All right! This is an episode of civil self-determination. That's it. If you like this episode. Feel free to subscribe and share it with us! If you have the ability to do so. can also join our membership. Or you can press the button. Thank you very much! Our DC group is now open! You are welcome to come in and chat with us. My Study Notes. See you next time.

Bye-bye!

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GxITK3Z-WQs

全民公投是一種體現民意的方式

也是直接民主的展現

在民主國家的選民

像是修憲、主權歸屬跟社會制度

目前的臺灣

已經就民生議題

舉行了16次公投

所以在我們中華民國

從能源、民生、法律等不同議題

都可以交由全民公投表決

這類的重要公共議題

都可以透過公投來解決

今天我們就來介紹一下

不同社會體制下的公投

#台灣 #臺灣 #公投 #中華民國 #中國 #同婚法 #公民投票 #公民自決 #美國 #51州 #憲法 #憲改 #修改憲法 #公民議題 #公民 #社會 #歷史 #全球 #國際 #國際觀 #英文 #全球化

📍讓我們繼續活下去 ► http://bit.ly/2tGL3eB

LINE貼圖搜尋「我的學習筆記」或是點下方連結

我的學習筆記2

https://reurl.cc/qkk38y

我的學習筆記3

https://reurl.cc/n77Rn6

我的學習筆記4

https://reurl.cc/mllNq9

🎬更多的學習筆記

YOUTUBE長影片討論 ► https://reurl.cc/gy2ZR

Tiktok上更多短影片 ► https://reurl.cc/bkjjz3

Facebook關注更多消息 ► https://bit.ly/2TFDZXC

Instagram掌握國際大事 ► https://reurl.cc/GyxEp

方格子學術公眾化成果 ► http://bit.ly/32J6mrK

DisCord群組一起聊天抬槓►https://discord.gg/YmSkbSVrUC

小額支付贊助我歐付寶►http://bit.ly/2Z1s1yA

當然還有PayPal►PayPal.Me/wMyNotebook

😉特別感謝5月份支持學習筆記的朋友

Polya Yang、柯誠譽、Rosemary 、阿里不打、Sion Ein、Roots Ko、Wu Pin-Ying、赤炎、懶虎提姆閣、Roy Li、KaHu Lau、葉小雲、李文傑、陳建宏、Louis Tseng、薩麥爾_MLBB、祈翠、Mariposa Bay、語禪、Krib Stek、張輔庭、whiskyhot886 、Chen Chong Lee、Charlie曾、蔡岳峰、Chung-Ming Wang、上善若水、林雋凱

📍特別感謝每位默默支持我們的朋友們，你們給的不只是錢財，更是一段友誼 當你像小蝦米一樣對抗大鯨魚，當你的敵人遠比你想像中強大的時候 發現了這個世界，其實有一群未曾相識卻力挺你的朋友，我想，這就是世界上最美好的感覺了
item.description:
Hello! Hello, everyone! I'm a niu ge. We've talked about the abolitionist controversy before. But in fact, in the Republic of China. There is no conclusion on the abolition of the death penalty! Chiu Siu-hong of the KMT even said that. A referendum should be held to decide to abolish the death penalty! And at about the same time. Vietnam also on April 23rd. for “inciting subversion of human rights”. Sentencing of 10 Defendants. 4 to 13 years imprisonment. The reason is that these people are calling for a referendum. To determine the future of Vietnam. A referendum is a way of realizing the will of the people. It's also a demonstration of direct democracy. Voters in a democracy. It's like a constitutional amendment,. Sovereignty, and a social system. Important public issues like this. can be resolved by referendum. Today we're going to introduce. Referendums under different social systems. Let's start with the old countries. Britain's Brexit Referendum. Starting with the series of referendums it triggered. We're going to talk about the referendums. Then we have to talk about the United Kingdom. When Britain was in the process of leaving the European Union. The referendum held in 2016. This referendum is not mandatory. But it's still the result of public opinion. It's been presented to the public. The EU Withdrawal Bill. Under this bill. Britain will leave the EU in 2019. So why does the UK want to leave the EU? That's a pretty big question. It covers political, economic and social aspects. It's like someone might think. Britain has lost a lot of autonomy and decision-making power. Because it had to be obeyed. Some of the regulations and policies set by the European Union. They believe that EU laws and regulations. have too much influence on British policy making. and limited the ability of the state to make its own decisions. Or the European Union's free movement policy. AKA citizens of the EU. Can move freely within the European Union. Dissatisfaction with work and residence. Thinking that it leads to too many immigrants. Affects employment opportunities for locals. And that's what most of the Brits who supported Brexit did. That's the main reason why they support Brexit. In England and Wales. People are more likely to support Brexit. But Scotland and Northern Ireland. Most people want to stay in the EU. Leaders in Northern Ireland. And even decided to take Northern Ireland. Is it going to leave the United Kingdom? A referendum was also held. History. Northern Ireland has also been criticized for. The question of whether or not to accept British jurisdiction. The two sides have been in turmoil for a long time. 1998. Belfast Agreement and EU Accession.

It's really over. 30 years of violent conflict. Now Britain is out of the European Union. Triggering the terms of the Belfast Agreement. There's a clause in this agreement that says. If it is clear that the majority of the people. Of Northern Ireland want to. Northern Ireland splits from Britain. The British and Irish Governments. A referendum should be organized. The Other Side of England. Scotland is still seeking a referendum on independence. Back in 2014. Scotland for the first time. Referendum on Independence. The result was 44.2% to 55.8%. Failed to declare independence. There is still a group of people in Scotland. looking for a way out of Britain. June 2022. Scottish Local Government Program. Second Independence Referendum in 2023. Rejected by British Prime Minister Johnson. Supreme Court Ruling. Not Authorized by the UK Government. Scotland is not authorized to. A referendum on leaving the United Kingdom. But unlike Northern Ireland and Scotland. Gibraltar, also a British territory. They wanted to stay in the UK through a referendum. Gibraltar is a British overseas territory. It's only about 6 square kilometers in size. The landmass is surrounded by Spain. Spain keeps claiming. He had sovereignty over Gibraltar. 1954. Spain restricts water supply to Gibraltar. Trying to regain sovereignty over Gibraltar. The result was that in 1967. Gibraltar held its first ever referendum. The result was in favor of the transfer of sovereignty to Spain. Only 44 votes. And those in favor of British sovereignty. There are 12,138 votes. After this. The British government also tried to. To return Gibraltar to Spain. Resulting in the return of Gibraltar to Spain in 2002. Another referendum was held. But in the end, it ended in. 98.97% of the votes were against the motion. Allowed Britain to maintain its rule in Gibraltar. But in fact, Britain was. It's made up of four different countries. and overseas territories. All of these places have the right to pass a referendum. to decide whether or not they want to stay in the UK. Just like the UK's referendum on leaving the EU. I'm done with Britain. Let's talk about it. Referendums in other democracies. Actually, something similar. It happened in Puerto Rico, in the Caribbean. As early as 1898. The U.S. was in the Spanish-American War. After winning the Spanish War. This former Spanish colony.

It fell into American hands. And then in 1917. Legislation was enacted and then formalized. Puerto Ricans are U.S. citizens. And also to help Puerto Rico set up a system of government. Including the Governor, the chief executive, who is directly elected by the people. And the bicameral legislature. 139 00:05:07,557 --> 00:05:09,767. Puerto Rico has a state militia. And armed forces similar to the state guard. Includes land, sea, and air forces. However. Restricted by the legal position of belonging. Puerto Rico in the U.S. Congress. There are no senators. There's only one non-voting delegate. Although Puerto Ricans don't have to carry passports. They can move freely throughout the U.S. States. You can also register as a voter in each state. But as long as the person is in Puerto Rico. You can't vote in the presidential election. You can only express your opinion in the party primaries. And candidates are usually here. After writing the policy check. I'm going to bring the campaign back to the home states. It's been over 100 years. American Puerto Ricans. Empty Citizenship. Without the full rights of citizenship. Except for the right to vote. Their local income. No Federal Income Tax. But you can't apply for it either. Some Federal Social Welfare Assistance. It's about becoming a state of the United States. It's better to be independent. It's been a controversial topic for decades. The idea of “independence” or “statehood”. Also a different local party. Key issues that need to be addressed. Last seen in November 2020. With 53% of voters. In favor of becoming a U.S. state. 47% of voters oppose. Deciding whether to become the 51st state of the United States. A slight majority in favor. Similar to the trend over the years. But this result. It needs to be passed by the U.S. Congress. To become a state of the United States. So these referendums. They're not legally enforceable. It only has the effect of tabula rasa. It's just a reference for Congress. 185 00:06:40,983 --> 00:06:42,819. But in December of 2022. Just before the end of the congressional session. The House of Representatives passed the Puerto Rico Status Act. The bill proposes that Puerto Rico. Hold Another Referendum. To determine the political positioning of Puerto Rico. There are three options. Become a state of the United States. Independence. Or after independence. Free association with the United States. The point is. For the first time in history. Giving mandatory force to the referendum result.

Whatever the outcome, the U.S. Congress. The choice of the Puerto Ricans will be honored. Let's move on to the next point. Another interesting country. That's Quebec, Canada. Quebec, Canada. Actually, something similar happened! What makes Quebec so special. A colonial history of the past. It's a very close relationship. The major ethnic groups in Quebec. are Catholics of French descent. With the majority of other Christian Englishmen. In language, culture, religion, and blood. There is a clear difference. Originally Canada's Constitution Act 1982. Bypassing Quebec for Constitutional Amendments. Quebec Considers New Constitution. does not protect the rights of the French-speaking population. Quebec is only considered a province of Canada. No special status for “Britain and France”. Then Premier of Quebec. Lévesque refused to sign the Canadian Constitution. Although the court said. that the Canadian Constitution applies automatically. To the entire country. But the federal government, in order to resolve this. A constitutional crisis involving Quebec's feelings of independence. Negotiations with the provinces for a constitutional amendment. The 1987 Lake Michigan Accord. The Canadian government delegates a lot of power. Including the power to veto constitutional amendments. Nomination of Supreme Court Judges. Developing Immigration Policy. Financial Aid and More. Since Quebec is not the only one to benefit from the program. The premiers of the provinces. Also accepted these proposals with open arms. The Parti Québécois, which advocates for Quebec's independence. In the 1994 Quebec general election. Won the Ruling Party. After the Québécois came to power. And then this referendum was called. In October of 1995. On “Whether or not Quebec secedes from Canada. Becoming an independent country. A referendum was held. Result 49.42% of voters. Recognized that Quebec should secede from Canada. 50.58% of voters oppose. In a democracy. Citizens can pass a referendum. Deciding whether to secede from a country. Or join another country. The people who live in this land. Have the right to self-determination by referendum. To decide on this piece of land. Sovereignty, social system. But another kind of social system. is not so lucky. Since there is a normal, legal procedure for referendums. Of course there's coerced referendums. And now for the next part. The referendum on Crimea. Early 2014. Russia sends unidentified militants. Occupied Ukraine.

After the Crimean Autonomous Republic. A referendum was also held. It was decided that Crimea would remain in Ukraine. Or to join Russia. There are two questions in the referendum. First. Do you support Crimea? To become a territory of the Russian Federation. Second. Are you in favor of resuming. Crimea's 1992 Constitution. Both options are to secede from Ukraine. But Ukraine and most of the UN member states 277. do not recognize the referendum. Considers it a violation of Ukraine's territorial integrity. and adopted United Nations General Assembly Resolution 68/262. Recognizing the referendum as null and void. Calling on countries not to recognize. The result of this referendum. February 2022. Russia invades Ukraine. Occupation of Ukrainian territory. Donetsk, Luhansk. Zaporozhye, Kherson. September 2022. Russia has initiated referendums in these four cities. At the time of the referendum. Military action between Russia and Ukraine. It's still going on. Most of the population of the four cities. have fled. The U.N. condemns the referendum as a violation. Of the U.N. Charter. There is only one country, North Korea. Accepted the validity of the referendum result. No other sovereign state accepts it. Finished with the West. Let's talk about the Chinese world. Actually, it's something like this. A referendum on national sovereignty. Not only in the Western world! In fact, there are referendums in the Chinese world as well. It's like after the end of World War II. Britain was in great decline. The British government is seriously considering. From Southeast Asia, including Singapore. Making a Gorgeous Turn. September 1962. Singapore was held. Referendum on Accession to the Articles of Confederation of Malaysia. The referendum was initiated by then Prime Minister Lee Kuan Yew. The referendum offered three choices. 1. Partial Autonomy. 2. Full Merger. 3. Reference to the treatment of East Malaysia. In the end, 71% of the voters chose. Partial autonomy option with maximum autonomy. According to the result of the referendum. Singapore in September 1963. Formal merger with Malaysia. Marking the birth of the Federation of Malaysia. Until 1965. Singapore was expelled from the Federation of Malaysia. Becoming an independent country. But that's another story. It was also a British colony. The Hong Kong government has held official referendums.

Some political parties have also organized unofficial referendums. So let's start with the official referendum. Early Hong Kong. Having a Council Outside the Government. Responsible for cleaning up Hong Kong. That's the Cleaning Authority. That's the predecessor of the Urban Council. The Cleansing Bureau was established as early as 1883. In the 19th century. To be responsible for the hygiene and prevention. Of epidemic in Hong Kong. It was also one of the few. Elected Public Bodies. But in fact, in the early days of its existence. The Cleansing Authority was also an official governmental. Controlled by Members of the House of Representatives. A battleground between the government and the citizenry. This tug-of-war eventually forced the authorities to. In 1896. In the case of the Cleansing Authority,. It should be guarded by the Crown. or unofficial Members in the majority. Holding a referendum. The result is a large majority of voters. Agree that the Clean Council should be dominated. By unofficial members. Although the voting result is different from the government's idea. The Cleansing Authority had no choice but to maintain. Of the six non-official members. 2 were directly elected. This referendum. Hong Kong people for the first time. Trying out the experience of elected municipal councils. Another Referendum in Hong Kong. It's the Civic Party of Hong Kong. And the League of Social Democrats. The two political parties in 2009. To fight for genuine universal suffrage. A political movement for the realization of universal suffrage. In the five Hong Kong Legislative Council constituencies. One pan-democrat in each district. Resignation of Members of the Legislative Council of Hong Kong. Creation of Five Vacant Seats. and then in accordance. With the Hong Kong Legislative Council Ordinance. A by-election must be held. The League of Social Democrats and the Civic Party. Proposed in the Legislative Council. “Motion on ”Resignation en masse of Members returned from five geographical constituencies as a referendum. Motion ultimately defeated. January 2010. Formal Resignation of Five Legislative Councillors. April of the same year. Five Resigning Members Re-apply for Election. The result is that the five resigning Members. Winning by a large margin of votes. Returned to the Legislative Council. Resignation en masse in five districts. Voting on the issue of “universal suffrage. Five Resigning Members. Returning to the Legislative Council. With a large percentage of votes. Considered as public opinion in favor of genuine universal suffrage. Also extremely controversial. Although Hong Kong has not legislated for referendum. But there have been referendums. In both the government and the public.

According to the UN Charter and international law. In the absence of external oppression or interference. The people are free to decide. Their political status. And free to pursue their economic. Social and cultural development. It's also what we usually call the right to self-determination. So much for that. Finally, let's go back to the Republic of China. Unlike the colonial society of Hong Kong. When the Republic of China was founded. In fact, it gave citizens the right to vote. It's a kind of referendum like in Western politics. A direct decision by the people. Modifying or Repealing a Law. A power to administer the law. But it's not very specific. So it's been a long time in dispute. The Constitution of the Republic. Of China is a mere article in the Constitution. No clear procedure for implementation. In 2018. After amendment of the Referendum Act. Returning the Right to Vote to Referendum. Current Taiwan. Already on the issue of people's livelihood. 16 referendums have been held. Including the most recent 16th case. For the purpose of whether to repeal Article 95(1) of the Electricity Law. That is to say, to abolish “nuclear power generating facilities”. Before the 114th year of the Republic of China. The “Stop All Operations” Requirement. 54.83% turnout. Of which 29.84% agree. Finally passed. Other topics are like. In forms other than civil marriage. Protecting Same-Sex Couples. The right to operate a permanent joint life. Under the full name of (Taiwan). Apply for all international sports events. and 2020 Tokyo Olympics. Prohibition of the opening of Fukushima,. 311 Importation of Agricultural Products and Foodstuffs. From Nuclear Disaster-Related Areas. And the subject of these. All of these issues have been put to a referendum. In the Republic of China. So in the R.O.C. From energy, people's livelihood, law, and other issues. All of them can be put to a referendum. The independence of Taiwan. Couldn't it also be realized through a referendum? And would it be unconstitutional or not? If you're interested. We'll come back to this later. All right! This is an episode of civil self-determination. That's it. If you like this episode. Feel free to subscribe and share it with us! If you have the ability to do so. can also join our membership. Or you can press the button. Thank you very much! Our DC group is now open! You are welcome to come in and chat with us. My Study Notes. See you next time.

Bye-bye!

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GxITK3Z-WQs

全民公投是一種體現民意的方式

也是直接民主的展現

在民主國家的選民

像是修憲、主權歸屬跟社會制度

目前的臺灣

已經就民生議題

舉行了16次公投

所以在我們中華民國

從能源、民生、法律等不同議題

都可以交由全民公投表決

這類的重要公共議題

都可以透過公投來解決

今天我們就來介紹一下

不同社會體制下的公投

#台灣 #臺灣 #公投 #中華民國 #中國 #同婚法 #公民投票 #公民自決 #美國 #51州 #憲法 #憲改 #修改憲法 #公民議題 #公民 #社會 #歷史 #全球 #國際 #國際觀 #英文 #全球化

📍讓我們繼續活下去 ► http://bit.ly/2tGL3eB

LINE貼圖搜尋「我的學習筆記」或是點下方連結

我的學習筆記2

https://reurl.cc/qkk38y

我的學習筆記3

https://reurl.cc/n77Rn6

我的學習筆記4

https://reurl.cc/mllNq9

🎬更多的學習筆記

YOUTUBE長影片討論 ► https://reurl.cc/gy2ZR

Tiktok上更多短影片 ► https://reurl.cc/bkjjz3

Facebook關注更多消息 ► https://bit.ly/2TFDZXC

Instagram掌握國際大事 ► https://reurl.cc/GyxEp

方格子學術公眾化成果 ► http://bit.ly/32J6mrK

DisCord群組一起聊天抬槓►https://discord.gg/YmSkbSVrUC

小額支付贊助我歐付寶►http://bit.ly/2Z1s1yA

當然還有PayPal►PayPal.Me/wMyNotebook

😉特別感謝5月份支持學習筆記的朋友

Polya Yang、柯誠譽、Rosemary 、阿里不打、Sion Ein、Roots Ko、Wu Pin-Ying、赤炎、懶虎提姆閣、Roy Li、KaHu Lau、葉小雲、李文傑、陳建宏、Louis Tseng、薩麥爾_MLBB、祈翠、Mariposa Bay、語禪、Krib Stek、張輔庭、whiskyhot886 、Chen Chong Lee、Charlie曾、蔡岳峰、Chung-Ming Wang、上善若水、林雋凱

📍特別感謝每位默默支持我們的朋友們，你們給的不只是錢財，更是一段友誼 當你像小蝦米一樣對抗大鯨魚，當你的敵人遠比你想像中強大的時候 發現了這個世界，其實有一群未曾相識卻力挺你的朋友，我想，這就是世界上最美好的感覺了
item.content:encoded:
Hello! Hello, everyone! I'm a niu ge. We've talked about the abolitionist controversy before. But in fact, in the Republic of China. There is no conclusion on the abolition of the death penalty! Chiu Siu-hong of the KMT even said that. A referendum should be held to decide to abolish the death penalty! And at about the same time. Vietnam also on April 23rd. for “inciting subversion of human rights”. Sentencing of 10 Defendants. 4 to 13 years imprisonment. The reason is that these people are calling for a referendum. To determine the future of Vietnam. A referendum is a way of realizing the will of the people. It's also a demonstration of direct democracy. Voters in a democracy. It's like a constitutional amendment,. Sovereignty, and a social system. Important public issues like this. can be resolved by referendum. Today we're going to introduce. Referendums under different social systems. Let's start with the old countries. Britain's Brexit Referendum. Starting with the series of referendums it triggered. We're going to talk about the referendums. Then we have to talk about the United Kingdom. When Britain was in the process of leaving the European Union. The referendum held in 2016. This referendum is not mandatory. But it's still the result of public opinion. It's been presented to the public. The EU Withdrawal Bill. Under this bill. Britain will leave the EU in 2019. So why does the UK want to leave the EU? That's a pretty big question. It covers political, economic and social aspects. It's like someone might think. Britain has lost a lot of autonomy and decision-making power. Because it had to be obeyed. Some of the regulations and policies set by the European Union. They believe that EU laws and regulations. have too much influence on British policy making. and limited the ability of the state to make its own decisions. Or the European Union's free movement policy. AKA citizens of the EU. Can move freely within the European Union. Dissatisfaction with work and residence. Thinking that it leads to too many immigrants. Affects employment opportunities for locals. And that's what most of the Brits who supported Brexit did. That's the main reason why they support Brexit. In England and Wales. People are more likely to support Brexit. But Scotland and Northern Ireland. Most people want to stay in the EU. Leaders in Northern Ireland. And even decided to take Northern Ireland. Is it going to leave the United Kingdom? A referendum was also held. History. Northern Ireland has also been criticized for. The question of whether or not to accept British jurisdiction. The two sides have been in turmoil for a long time. 1998. Belfast Agreement and EU Accession.

It's really over. 30 years of violent conflict. Now Britain is out of the European Union. Triggering the terms of the Belfast Agreement. There's a clause in this agreement that says. If it is clear that the majority of the people. Of Northern Ireland want to. Northern Ireland splits from Britain. The British and Irish Governments. A referendum should be organized. The Other Side of England. Scotland is still seeking a referendum on independence. Back in 2014. Scotland for the first time. Referendum on Independence. The result was 44.2% to 55.8%. Failed to declare independence. There is still a group of people in Scotland. looking for a way out of Britain. June 2022. Scottish Local Government Program. Second Independence Referendum in 2023. Rejected by British Prime Minister Johnson. Supreme Court Ruling. Not Authorized by the UK Government. Scotland is not authorized to. A referendum on leaving the United Kingdom. But unlike Northern Ireland and Scotland. Gibraltar, also a British territory. They wanted to stay in the UK through a referendum. Gibraltar is a British overseas territory. It's only about 6 square kilometers in size. The landmass is surrounded by Spain. Spain keeps claiming. He had sovereignty over Gibraltar. 1954. Spain restricts water supply to Gibraltar. Trying to regain sovereignty over Gibraltar. The result was that in 1967. Gibraltar held its first ever referendum. The result was in favor of the transfer of sovereignty to Spain. Only 44 votes. And those in favor of British sovereignty. There are 12,138 votes. After this. The British government also tried to. To return Gibraltar to Spain. Resulting in the return of Gibraltar to Spain in 2002. Another referendum was held. But in the end, it ended in. 98.97% of the votes were against the motion. Allowed Britain to maintain its rule in Gibraltar. But in fact, Britain was. It's made up of four different countries. and overseas territories. All of these places have the right to pass a referendum. to decide whether or not they want to stay in the UK. Just like the UK's referendum on leaving the EU. I'm done with Britain. Let's talk about it. Referendums in other democracies. Actually, something similar. It happened in Puerto Rico, in the Caribbean. As early as 1898. The U.S. was in the Spanish-American War. After winning the Spanish War. This former Spanish colony.

It fell into American hands. And then in 1917. Legislation was enacted and then formalized. Puerto Ricans are U.S. citizens. And also to help Puerto Rico set up a system of government. Including the Governor, the chief executive, who is directly elected by the people. And the bicameral legislature. 139 00:05:07,557 --> 00:05:09,767. Puerto Rico has a state militia. And armed forces similar to the state guard. Includes land, sea, and air forces. However. Restricted by the legal position of belonging. Puerto Rico in the U.S. Congress. There are no senators. There's only one non-voting delegate. Although Puerto Ricans don't have to carry passports. They can move freely throughout the U.S. States. You can also register as a voter in each state. But as long as the person is in Puerto Rico. You can't vote in the presidential election. You can only express your opinion in the party primaries. And candidates are usually here. After writing the policy check. I'm going to bring the campaign back to the home states. It's been over 100 years. American Puerto Ricans. Empty Citizenship. Without the full rights of citizenship. Except for the right to vote. Their local income. No Federal Income Tax. But you can't apply for it either. Some Federal Social Welfare Assistance. It's about becoming a state of the United States. It's better to be independent. It's been a controversial topic for decades. The idea of “independence” or “statehood”. Also a different local party. Key issues that need to be addressed. Last seen in November 2020. With 53% of voters. In favor of becoming a U.S. state. 47% of voters oppose. Deciding whether to become the 51st state of the United States. A slight majority in favor. Similar to the trend over the years. But this result. It needs to be passed by the U.S. Congress. To become a state of the United States. So these referendums. They're not legally enforceable. It only has the effect of tabula rasa. It's just a reference for Congress. 185 00:06:40,983 --> 00:06:42,819. But in December of 2022. Just before the end of the congressional session. The House of Representatives passed the Puerto Rico Status Act. The bill proposes that Puerto Rico. Hold Another Referendum. To determine the political positioning of Puerto Rico. There are three options. Become a state of the United States. Independence. Or after independence. Free association with the United States. The point is. For the first time in history. Giving mandatory force to the referendum result.

Whatever the outcome, the U.S. Congress. The choice of the Puerto Ricans will be honored. Let's move on to the next point. Another interesting country. That's Quebec, Canada. Quebec, Canada. Actually, something similar happened! What makes Quebec so special. A colonial history of the past. It's a very close relationship. The major ethnic groups in Quebec. are Catholics of French descent. With the majority of other Christian Englishmen. In language, culture, religion, and blood. There is a clear difference. Originally Canada's Constitution Act 1982. Bypassing Quebec for Constitutional Amendments. Quebec Considers New Constitution. does not protect the rights of the French-speaking population. Quebec is only considered a province of Canada. No special status for “Britain and France”. Then Premier of Quebec. Lévesque refused to sign the Canadian Constitution. Although the court said. that the Canadian Constitution applies automatically. To the entire country. But the federal government, in order to resolve this. A constitutional crisis involving Quebec's feelings of independence. Negotiations with the provinces for a constitutional amendment. The 1987 Lake Michigan Accord. The Canadian government delegates a lot of power. Including the power to veto constitutional amendments. Nomination of Supreme Court Judges. Developing Immigration Policy. Financial Aid and More. Since Quebec is not the only one to benefit from the program. The premiers of the provinces. Also accepted these proposals with open arms. The Parti Québécois, which advocates for Quebec's independence. In the 1994 Quebec general election. Won the Ruling Party. After the Québécois came to power. And then this referendum was called. In October of 1995. On “Whether or not Quebec secedes from Canada. Becoming an independent country. A referendum was held. Result 49.42% of voters. Recognized that Quebec should secede from Canada. 50.58% of voters oppose. In a democracy. Citizens can pass a referendum. Deciding whether to secede from a country. Or join another country. The people who live in this land. Have the right to self-determination by referendum. To decide on this piece of land. Sovereignty, social system. But another kind of social system. is not so lucky. Since there is a normal, legal procedure for referendums. Of course there's coerced referendums. And now for the next part. The referendum on Crimea. Early 2014. Russia sends unidentified militants. Occupied Ukraine.

After the Crimean Autonomous Republic. A referendum was also held. It was decided that Crimea would remain in Ukraine. Or to join Russia. There are two questions in the referendum. First. Do you support Crimea? To become a territory of the Russian Federation. Second. Are you in favor of resuming. Crimea's 1992 Constitution. Both options are to secede from Ukraine. But Ukraine and most of the UN member states 277. do not recognize the referendum. Considers it a violation of Ukraine's territorial integrity. and adopted United Nations General Assembly Resolution 68/262. Recognizing the referendum as null and void. Calling on countries not to recognize. The result of this referendum. February 2022. Russia invades Ukraine. Occupation of Ukrainian territory. Donetsk, Luhansk. Zaporozhye, Kherson. September 2022. Russia has initiated referendums in these four cities. At the time of the referendum. Military action between Russia and Ukraine. It's still going on. Most of the population of the four cities. have fled. The U.N. condemns the referendum as a violation. Of the U.N. Charter. There is only one country, North Korea. Accepted the validity of the referendum result. No other sovereign state accepts it. Finished with the West. Let's talk about the Chinese world. Actually, it's something like this. A referendum on national sovereignty. Not only in the Western world! In fact, there are referendums in the Chinese world as well. It's like after the end of World War II. Britain was in great decline. The British government is seriously considering. From Southeast Asia, including Singapore. Making a Gorgeous Turn. September 1962. Singapore was held. Referendum on Accession to the Articles of Confederation of Malaysia. The referendum was initiated by then Prime Minister Lee Kuan Yew. The referendum offered three choices. 1. Partial Autonomy. 2. Full Merger. 3. Reference to the treatment of East Malaysia. In the end, 71% of the voters chose. Partial autonomy option with maximum autonomy. According to the result of the referendum. Singapore in September 1963. Formal merger with Malaysia. Marking the birth of the Federation of Malaysia. Until 1965. Singapore was expelled from the Federation of Malaysia. Becoming an independent country. But that's another story. It was also a British colony. The Hong Kong government has held official referendums.

Some political parties have also organized unofficial referendums. So let's start with the official referendum. Early Hong Kong. Having a Council Outside the Government. Responsible for cleaning up Hong Kong. That's the Cleaning Authority. That's the predecessor of the Urban Council. The Cleansing Bureau was established as early as 1883. In the 19th century. To be responsible for the hygiene and prevention. Of epidemic in Hong Kong. It was also one of the few. Elected Public Bodies. But in fact, in the early days of its existence. The Cleansing Authority was also an official governmental. Controlled by Members of the House of Representatives. A battleground between the government and the citizenry. This tug-of-war eventually forced the authorities to. In 1896. In the case of the Cleansing Authority,. It should be guarded by the Crown. or unofficial Members in the majority. Holding a referendum. The result is a large majority of voters. Agree that the Clean Council should be dominated. By unofficial members. Although the voting result is different from the government's idea. The Cleansing Authority had no choice but to maintain. Of the six non-official members. 2 were directly elected. This referendum. Hong Kong people for the first time. Trying out the experience of elected municipal councils. Another Referendum in Hong Kong. It's the Civic Party of Hong Kong. And the League of Social Democrats. The two political parties in 2009. To fight for genuine universal suffrage. A political movement for the realization of universal suffrage. In the five Hong Kong Legislative Council constituencies. One pan-democrat in each district. Resignation of Members of the Legislative Council of Hong Kong. Creation of Five Vacant Seats. and then in accordance. With the Hong Kong Legislative Council Ordinance. A by-election must be held. The League of Social Democrats and the Civic Party. Proposed in the Legislative Council. “Motion on ”Resignation en masse of Members returned from five geographical constituencies as a referendum. Motion ultimately defeated. January 2010. Formal Resignation of Five Legislative Councillors. April of the same year. Five Resigning Members Re-apply for Election. The result is that the five resigning Members. Winning by a large margin of votes. Returned to the Legislative Council. Resignation en masse in five districts. Voting on the issue of “universal suffrage. Five Resigning Members. Returning to the Legislative Council. With a large percentage of votes. Considered as public opinion in favor of genuine universal suffrage. Also extremely controversial. Although Hong Kong has not legislated for referendum. But there have been referendums. In both the government and the public.

According to the UN Charter and international law. In the absence of external oppression or interference. The people are free to decide. Their political status. And free to pursue their economic. Social and cultural development. It's also what we usually call the right to self-determination. So much for that. Finally, let's go back to the Republic of China. Unlike the colonial society of Hong Kong. When the Republic of China was founded. In fact, it gave citizens the right to vote. It's a kind of referendum like in Western politics. A direct decision by the people. Modifying or Repealing a Law. A power to administer the law. But it's not very specific. So it's been a long time in dispute. The Constitution of the Republic. Of China is a mere article in the Constitution. No clear procedure for implementation. In 2018. After amendment of the Referendum Act. Returning the Right to Vote to Referendum. Current Taiwan. Already on the issue of people's livelihood. 16 referendums have been held. Including the most recent 16th case. For the purpose of whether to repeal Article 95(1) of the Electricity Law. That is to say, to abolish “nuclear power generating facilities”. Before the 114th year of the Republic of China. The “Stop All Operations” Requirement. 54.83% turnout. Of which 29.84% agree. Finally passed. Other topics are like. In forms other than civil marriage. Protecting Same-Sex Couples. The right to operate a permanent joint life. Under the full name of (Taiwan). Apply for all international sports events. and 2020 Tokyo Olympics. Prohibition of the opening of Fukushima,. 311 Importation of Agricultural Products and Foodstuffs. From Nuclear Disaster-Related Areas. And the subject of these. All of these issues have been put to a referendum. In the Republic of China. So in the R.O.C. From energy, people's livelihood, law, and other issues. All of them can be put to a referendum. The independence of Taiwan. Couldn't it also be realized through a referendum? And would it be unconstitutional or not? If you're interested. We'll come back to this later. All right! This is an episode of civil self-determination. That's it. If you like this episode. Feel free to subscribe and share it with us! If you have the ability to do so. can also join our membership. Or you can press the button. Thank you very much! Our DC group is now open! You are welcome to come in and chat with us. My Study Notes. See you next time.

Bye-bye!

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GxITK3Z-WQs

全民公投是一種體現民意的方式

也是直接民主的展現

在民主國家的選民

像是修憲、主權歸屬跟社會制度

目前的臺灣

已經就民生議題

舉行了16次公投

所以在我們中華民國

從能源、民生、法律等不同議題

都可以交由全民公投表決

這類的重要公共議題

都可以透過公投來解決

今天我們就來介紹一下

不同社會體制下的公投

#台灣 #臺灣 #公投 #中華民國 #中國 #同婚法 #公民投票 #公民自決 #美國 #51州 #憲法 #憲改 #修改憲法 #公民議題 #公民 #社會 #歷史 #全球 #國際 #國際觀 #英文 #全球化

📍讓我們繼續活下去 ► http://bit.ly/2tGL3eB

LINE貼圖搜尋「我的學習筆記」或是點下方連結

https://reurl.cc/n77Rn6

https://reurl.cc/mllNq9

當然還有PayPal►PayPal.Me/wMyNotebook

😉特別感謝5月份支持學習筆記的朋友

Polya Yang、柯誠譽、Rosemary 、阿里不打、Sion Ein、Roots Ko、Wu Pin-Ying、赤炎、懶虎提姆閣、Roy Li、KaHu Lau、葉小雲、李文傑、陳建宏、Louis Tseng、薩麥爾_MLBB、祈翠、Mariposa Bay、語禪、Krib Stek、張輔庭、whiskyhot886 、Chen Chong Lee、Charlie曾、蔡岳峰、Chung-Ming Wang、上善若水、林雋凱

📍特別感謝每位默默支持我們的朋友們，你們給的不只是錢財，更是一段友誼 當你像小蝦米一樣對抗大鯨魚，當你的敵人遠比你想像中強大的時候 發現了這個世界，其實有一群未曾相識卻力挺你的朋友，我想，這就是世界上最美好的感覺了
